target.name: Sofar Ocean
target.founder: Tim Janssen, Brad Lassey, Alex Wight

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $75M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Sofar Ocean is a Series B stage company operating in Ocean & Maritime technology. The company has raised $75M+ to date. No direct public comparables exist in this subsector, and no private peer transactions were available for benchmarking at the time of this analysis.

Given the limited comp universe, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($75M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier—consistent with typical Series B to subsequent-stage transitions—yields an implied valuation of $142.5M. This multiplier reflects the capital intensity and risk profile typical of frontier-stage maritime technology ventures advancing toward later funding rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $121M to $164M, with a median point of $142M. The spread reflects sensitivity around the step-up multiplier and the appropriateness of using total raised as a base proxy. Key uncertainties include the absence of peer transaction data, which limits triangulation to a single method, and lack of visibility into unit economics, revenue traction, or deployment scale—variables that would typically inform a more granular DCF or comparable company approach. Further data on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and capital efficiency would narrow this range materially.
